seoTitle: How to Fix Data Source Errors in Power BI
metaDescription: Troubleshoot the most common causes of Power BI data source errors so your reports stay accurate and your dashboards keep working.
intro: A data source error can bring your reporting process to a halt right when accurate insights matter most. This guide walks through the most common causes of these errors in Power BI and how to resolve them. It's meant for anyone who has hit a broken connection or failed refresh and needs a clear path to fixing it.

steps.heading: Identify common causes of data source errors
steps.body: Start by narrowing down whether the issue is related to credentials, connection settings, or changes in the underlying source structure.

steps.heading: Check your data connection settings
steps.body: Review the connection details for your data source to confirm they're still valid and haven't changed since the report was built.

steps.heading: Follow steps to troubleshoot the error
steps.body: Work through the error message systematically, addressing credentials, permissions, or source availability as needed.

steps.heading: Maintain data integrity going forward
steps.body: Once resolved, put practices in place, like monitoring scheduled refreshes, to catch similar issues before they affect your reports.

steps.heading: Consider automation to reduce future errors
steps.body: Where possible, use scheduled refreshes and integrations that reduce the chance of manual errors interrupting your data connections.

faq.question: What usually causes a data source error in Power BI?
faq.answer: Common causes include expired credentials, changed source structures, network issues, or permission changes on the underlying data source.

faq.question: How do I know if the problem is with Power BI or my data source?
faq.answer: Checking the specific error message and testing the connection directly against the source can help determine where the problem originates.

faq.question: Can scheduled refresh failures be prevented?
faq.answer: Not entirely, but keeping credentials updated and monitoring source changes reduces the likelihood of refresh failures.

faq.question: Do I need IT support to fix every data source error?
faq.answer: Not always; many errors can be resolved by updating credentials or connection settings directly, though some require broader access to fix.

faq.question: Why does data integrity matter for business intelligence?
faq.answer: Inaccurate or broken connections can lead to flawed reports, which directly affects the quality of business decisions made from that data.
